title: SSC CGL T-I Quant Section Test

questions.question: A 1.5 m tall boy observes a pole. The angle of elevation of the top of the pole is 30‚ąė30^\circ30‚ąė‚Äč from the eyes of the boy. Then he approaches the pole by 7.3 m. Now the angle of elevation is 45‚ąė45^\circ45‚ąė. Find the height of the pole from the ground. Take 3=1.73 \sqrt{3} = 1.733‚Äč=1.73‚Äč
questions.explanation: Given: Height of boy‚Äôs eyes = 1.5 m Initial angle of elevation = 30¬į 30\degree30¬į‚Äč‚Äč Distance reduced = 7.3 m New angle of elevation = 45¬į45\degree45¬į‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Let the height of the pole be x m and the distance of the boy from the pole be y m. According to the question ‚Äčtan‚Ā°30‚ąė=x‚ąí1.5yy3=x‚ąí1.5y1.73=x‚ąí1.5x=y1.73+1.5‚ÄĒ-(1)tan‚Ā°45‚ąė=x‚ąí1.5y‚ąí7.3y‚ąí7.3=x‚ąí1.5y‚ąí5.8=x‚ÄĒ-(2)\tan 30^\circ = \frac{x - 1.5}{y} \\[6pt] \frac{y}{\sqrt{3}} = x - 1.5 \\[6pt] \frac{y}{1.73} = x - 1.5 \\[6pt] x = \frac{y}{1.73} + 1.5 \quad \text{----(1)} \\[10pt]\tan 45^\circ = \frac{x - 1.5}{y - 7.3} \\[6pt]y - 7.3 = x - 1.5 \\[6pt]y - 5.8 = x \quad \text{----(2)}tan30‚ąė=yx‚ąí1.5‚Äč3‚Äčy‚Äč=x‚ąí1.51.73y‚Äč=x‚ąí1.5x=1.73y‚Äč+1.5‚ÄĒ-(1)tan45‚ąė=y‚ąí7.3x‚ąí1.5‚Äčy‚ąí7.3=x‚ąí1.5y‚ąí5.8=x‚ÄĒ-(2)‚Äč‚Äč Put value of x from (1), in (2) =&gt y ‚Äď 5.8 = y1.73\frac {y}{1.73}1.73y‚Äč‚Äč + 1.5 =&gt 0.42y = 7.3 =&gt y = 17.3 m ‚ąī x = 17.3 ‚Äď 5.8 = 11.5 m

questions.question: Raghuvir Obtained an average of 25 marks in 12 tests. Rumela has been maintaining an average of 23 marks so far, but she has taken only 8 of the tests. How much does Rumela have to score on an average in the remaining 4 tests to match Raghuvir‚Äôs performance?
questions.explanation: Given: Raghuvir obtained an average of 25 marks in 12 tests. Rumela has been maintaining an average of 23 marks so far. She has taken only 8 of the tests. Formula Used: Total weight = Average weight √ó Number of boxes ‚ÄčAverage=Number of ScoresNumber of Tests\text{Average} = \frac{\text{Number of Scores}}{\text{Number of Tests}}Average=Number of TestsNumber of Scores‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Marks scored by Rahuvir in 12 tests = 25 √ó 12= 300 Marks scored by Rumela in first 8 tests = 23 √ó 8 = 184 Marks required by Rumela in the next 4 tests to match the average of Raghuvir = 300 ‚Äď 184 = 116 Required average =1164 \frac{116}44116‚Äč‚Äč = 29

questions.question: ‚ÄčA certain sum doubles itself in 8 years on simple interest per annum. Find the rate percentage of the interest.‚Äč
questions.explanation: Given: A sum doubles itself in 8 years under Simple Interest. Formula Used: SI = (Principal √ó Rate √ó Time) 100\frac{\text{(Principal √ó Rate √ó Time) }}{ 100}100(Principal √ó Rate √ó Time) ‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: P = SI (because amount is double of principal) So, P =(P√óR√ó8)100\frac{ (P √ó R √ó 8) }{100}100(P√óR√ó8)‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč R = 1008\frac{100 } 88100‚Äč‚Äč = 12.5% Rate of interest = 12.5% per annum

questions.question: A cone of radius 3 cm is mounted on the base of a hemisphere of radius 7 cm. The height of the cone is twice the height of hemisphere. What is the resulting volume of the solid?
questions.explanation: Given: Hemisphere radius = 7 cm Cone radius = 3 cm Cone height = 2 √ó hemisphere height = 2 √ó 7 cm = 14 cm Formula Used: Volume of hemisphere = 23ŌÄr3\frac{2}{3} \pi r^332‚ÄčŌÄr3‚Äč‚Äč Volume of cone = 13ŌÄr2h\frac{1}{3} \pi r^2 h31‚ÄčŌÄr2h‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Total Volume = Hemisphere volume + Cone Volume ‚Äč=23ŌÄ(7)3+13ŌÄ(3)2(14) =13√ó227(2√ó343+126) =2221√ó(812) =850.67 cm3= \frac{2}{3} \pi (7)^3 + \frac{1}{3} \pi (3)^2 (14) \\ \ \\= \frac{1}3 \times \frac{22}{7}\left( 2 \times 343 + 126\right) \\ \ \\ = \frac{22}{21}\times\left(812\right) \\ \ \\ = 850.67 \ cm^3=32‚ÄčŌÄ(7)3+31‚ÄčŌÄ(3)2(14) =31‚Äč√ó722‚Äč(2√ó343+126) =2122‚Äč√ó(812) =850.67 cm3‚Äč‚Äč Thus, The total volume of the solid is approximately 850.67 cm¬≥

questions.question: A hollow metal sphere is of radius 5 m and thickness 20 cm. What is the approximate volume of metal required to make the sphere?
questions.explanation: Given: External radius R = 5 m Internal radius r = 4.8 m Formula Used: Volume of metal = 43ŌÄR3‚ąí43ŌÄr3\frac{4}{3} \pi R^3 - \frac{4}{3} \pi r^334‚ÄčŌÄR3‚ąí34‚ÄčŌÄr3‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Volume of metal = 43√ó227((5)3‚ąí(4.8)3)\frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \left( (5)^3 - (4.8)^3 \right)34‚Äč√ó722‚Äč((5)3‚ąí(4.8)3)‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč=43√ó227(125‚ąí110.592) =43√ó227√ó14.408 =1265.904221= \frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \left( 125 - 110.592 \right) \\ \ \\= \frac{4}{3} \times \frac{22}{7} \times 14.408 \\ \ \\ = \frac{1265.9042}{21}=34‚Äč√ó722‚Äč(125‚ąí110.592) =34‚Äč√ó722‚Äč√ó14.408 =211265.9042‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč = 60.38 m3

questions.question: The ratio of the interior and exterior angles of a regular polygon is 4 : 1. What is the number of sides of the polygon?
questions.explanation: Given: The ratio of the interior and exterior angles of a regular polygon = 4 : 1 Formula Used: Sum of interior and exterior angle for any polygon = 180¬į Interior angle of a regular polygon = (n‚ąí2)√ó180¬įn\frac{(n-2) \times 180¬į}{n}n(n‚ąí2)√ó180¬į‚Äč‚Äč, where n is the number of sides. Exterior angle of a regular polygon = 360¬įn\frac{360¬į}{n}n360¬į‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Given the ratio of interior to exterior angles is 4 : 1, So: ‚Äč(n‚ąí2)√ó180¬įn=4√ó360¬įn (n‚ąí2)√ó180¬į=4√ó360¬į (n‚ąí2)√ó180¬į=1440¬į n‚ąí2=1440¬į180¬į=8 n=8+2=10\frac{(n-2) \times 180¬į}{n} = 4 \times \frac{360¬į}{n} \\ \ \\(n-2) \times 180¬į = 4 \times 360¬į \\ \ \\(n-2) \times 180¬į = 1440¬į \\ \ \\n - 2 = \frac{1440¬į}{180¬į} = 8 \\ \ \\n = 8 + 2 = 10n(n‚ąí2)√ó180¬į‚Äč=4√ón360¬į‚Äč (n‚ąí2)√ó180¬į=4√ó360¬į (n‚ąí2)√ó180¬į=1440¬į n‚ąí2=180¬į1440¬į‚Äč=8 n=8+2=10‚Äč‚Äč Thus, the number of sides of the polygon is 10

questions.question: Two trains having lengths of 410 m and 390 m are running at speeds of 80 km/h and 100 km/h, respectively, in the same direction. The time taken (in minutes) by the faster train, coming from behind, to completely cross the other train is:
questions.explanation: Given: Length of first train = 410 m Length of second train = 390 m Speed of first train = 80 km/h Speed of second train = 100 km/h Concept Used: Relative Speed=Speed of faster train‚ąíSpeed of slower train Time=Sum of train lengthsRelative speed\text{Relative Speed} = \text{Speed of faster train} - \text{Speed of slower train}\\ \ \\\text{Time} = \frac{\text{Sum of train lengths}}{\text{Relative speed}}Relative Speed=Speed of faster train‚ąíSpeed of slower train Time=Relative speedSum of train lengths‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Length of both train = 390 + 410 = 800 Both train, same direction that's why Difference = 100 - 80 = 20 km/hr ‚ÄčTotal = Total Distance Total time√ó185\frac{\text{Total Distance }}{\text{Total time}}\times\frac{18}5 Total timeTotal Distance ‚Äč√ó518‚Äč‚Äč = 80020√ó185=144sec\frac{800}{20}\times\frac{18}5 = 144 sec20800‚Äč√ó518‚Äč=144sec‚Äč In 60 sec = 1 min 144 sec = 160√ó144=2.4min\frac{1}{60}\times 144 = 2.4 \text{min}601‚Äč√ó144=2.4min

questions.question: ABCDEF is a regular hexagon and m(BE)‚Äĺ\overline{(BE)}(BE)‚Äč‚Äč = 14 cm. What is the perimeter of the hexagon?
questions.explanation: Given: ABCDEF is a regular hexagon Length of diagonal BE = 14 cm Formula Used: Perimeter = 6 √ó side Solution: Given: BE = 14 cm Side =142= \frac{14}{2}=214‚Äč‚Äč = 7 cm Perimeter = 6 √ó 7 = 42 cm

questions.question: The selling price of an article is Rs 750 and the profit percentage is 25 percent. If the profit percentage is 35 percent, then what will be the selling price of the article?
questions.explanation: Given: Selling price (SP) = Rs. 750 at a profit of 25% To find: New selling price when the profit percentage is 35% Formula Used: SP = CP√ó(1+Profit %100)CP \times \left(1 + \frac{\text{Profit \%}}{100}\right)CP√ó(1+100Profit %‚Äč)‚Äč‚Äč CP = SP1+Profit %100\frac{SP}{1 + \frac{\text{Profit \%}}{100}}1+100Profit %‚ÄčSP‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Cost Price = 7501+25100=7501.25=600\frac{750}{1 + \frac{25}{100}} = \frac{750}{1.25} = 6001+10025‚Äč750‚Äč=1.25750‚Äč=600‚Äč‚Äč New SP = 600√ó(1+35100)=600√ó1.35=Rs. 810600 \times \left(1 + \frac{35}{100}\right) = 600 \times 1.35 = Rs. \ 810600√ó(1+10035‚Äč)=600√ó1.35=Rs. 810‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: ‚Äč‚ÄčSuhas travelled by train to cover 512\frac5{12}125‚Äč of the wourney, and then traveled by bus to cover 13\frac1331‚Äčof the wourney. After that he travelled the remaining 36 km on a bicycle. How much in all did Suhas travel?‚Äč
questions.explanation: Given: By train = 512\frac5{12}125‚Äč‚Äč of the wourney By bus =13\frac 1331‚Äč‚Äč of the wourney Remaining = 36 km (by bicycle) Solution: Fraction by train + fraction by bus = 512+13\frac5{12} + \frac13125‚Äč+31‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč =512+412\frac 5{12 }+ \frac4{12}125‚Äč+124‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč=912=34=\frac 9{12} = \frac34=129‚Äč=43‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Remaining wourney = 1 ‚Äď 34=14\frac34 = \frac1443‚Äč=41‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč So, 14\frac 1441‚Äč‚Äč of total = 36 Total = 36 √ó 4 = 144 km Total distance Suhas travelled = 144 km

questions.question: If sin‚Ā°őł+(14)cosec‚Ā°őł=1, and 0‚Č§őł‚Č§90‚ąė\sin\theta + \left(\tfrac{1}{4}\right)\cosec\theta = 1,\ \text{and}\ 0 \leq \theta \leq 90^\circsinőł+(41‚Äč)cosecőł=1, and 0‚Č§őł‚Č§90‚ąė, then find the value of őł.
questions.explanation: Given: ‚Äčsin‚Ā°őł+14cosec‚Ā°őł=1\sin \theta + \frac{1}{4} \cosec \theta = 1sinőł+41‚Äčcosecőł=1 Solution: We know that cosec‚Ā°őł=1sin‚Ā°őł\cosec \theta = \frac{1}{\sin \theta}cosecőł=sinőł1‚Äč‚Äč. Substituting this into the equation: ‚Äčsin‚Ā°őł+14√ó1sin‚Ā°őł=1\sin \theta + \frac{1}{4} \times \frac{1}{\sin \theta} = 1sinőł+41‚Äč√ósinőł1‚Äč=1‚Äč 4sin‚Ā°őł+1sin‚Ā°őł=4\sin \theta + \frac{1}{\sin \theta} = 4sinőł+sinőł1‚Äč=4‚Äč ‚Äč4sin‚Ā°2őł+1=4sin‚Ā°őł4\sin^2 \theta + 1 = 4 \sin \theta4sin2őł+1=4sinőł‚Äč ‚Äč4sin‚Ā°2őł‚ąí4sin‚Ā°őł+1=04 \sin^2 \theta - 4 \sin \theta + 1 = 04sin2őł‚ąí4sinőł+1=0‚Äč This is a quadratic equation in terms of sin‚Ā°őł.\sin \theta.sinőł.‚Äč Let x = sin‚Ā°őł \sin \thetasinőł‚Äč, so the equation becomes: ‚Äč4x2‚ąí4x+1=04x^2 - 4x + 1 = 04x2‚ąí4x+1=0‚Äč Now, solve this quadratic equation using the quadratic formula: x = ‚ąí(‚ąí4)¬Ī(‚ąí4)2‚ąí4‚čÖ4‚čÖ12√ó4\frac{-(-4) \pm \sqrt{(-4)^2 - 4 \cdot 4 \cdot 1}}{2 \times 4}2√ó4‚ąí(‚ąí4)¬Ī(‚ąí4)2‚ąí4‚čÖ4‚čÖ1‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč x =4¬Ī08 \frac{4 \pm 0}{8}84¬Ī0‚Äč‚Äč x =48=12 \frac{4}{8} = \frac{1}{2}84‚Äč=21‚Äč‚Äč Thus, sin‚Ā°őł=12\sin \theta = \frac{1}{2}sinőł=21‚Äč‚Äč. Since 0 ‚Č§őł‚Č§90‚ąė\leq \theta \leq 90^\circ‚Č§őł‚Č§90‚ąė‚Äč, we know that: ‚Äčőł=30‚ąė\theta = 30^\circőł=30‚ąė‚Äč

questions.question: Rawesh bought 5 kg of apples for 875 and sold it for 220 per kg. How much is the profit gained by him?
questions.explanation: ‚ÄčGiven: Cost price of 5 kg of apples = 875 Selling price per kg of apples = 220 Total quantity = 5 kg Formula Used: Profit = Total Selling Price ‚ąí Total Cost Price Where: Total Selling Price = Selling price per kg √ó Quantity sold Total Cost Price = Cost price for 5 kg Solution: Total Selling Price = 220 √ó 5 = 1100 Profit = 1100 - 875 = 225 Thus, the profit gained by Rawesh is 225.

questions.question: A toy weighing 24 grams of an alloy of two metals is worth Rs 174/-, but if the weights of the two metals be interchanged, the toy would be worth Rs 162/-. If the price of one metal be Rs. 8 per gram, find the price of the other metal used to make the toy.
questions.explanation: Given: Weight of toy = 24 g Price in first case = Rs. 174 Price in second case = Rs. 162 Price of one metal = Rs. 8 per g Solution: Let weight of metal 1 = x g and metal 2 = (24 ‚Äď x) g Cost in first case = 8x + p(24 ‚Äď x) Cost in second case = 8(24 ‚Äď x) + px Where p = price of second metal Equation 1: 8x + p(24 ‚Äď x) = 174 Equation 2: 8(24 ‚Äď x) + px = 162 Expanding eqn (1): 8x + 24p ‚Äď px = 174 ‚Ä¶ (i) Expanding eqn (2): 192 ‚Äď 8x + px = 162 ‚Äď8x + px = ‚Äď30 8x ‚Äď px = 30 ‚Ä¶ (ii) Now add (i) and (ii): (8x ‚Äď px) + (8x ‚Äď px) + 24p = 174 + 30 16x ‚Äď 2px + 24p = 204 But from (ii): 8x ‚Äď px = 30 So substitute: 2(8x ‚Äď px) + 24p = 204 2(30) + 24p = 204 60 + 24p = 204 24p = 144 p = 6 Price of the other metal = Rs. 6 per gram

questions.question: A dishonest shopkeeper promises to sell his goods at cost price. However, he uses a weight that actually weighs 37% less than what is written on it. Find his profit percentage.
questions.explanation: Given: Shopkeeper claims to sell at cost price He uses weights that are 37% less than the marked weight Concept Used: The shopkeeper gives less quantity than he claims, but charges full price. This results in a gain, which is calculated based on actual quantity given. Formula Used: Profit % = (GainCost Price)√ó100\left( \frac{\text{Gain}}{\text{Cost Price}} \right) \times 100(Cost PriceGain‚Äč)√ó100‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Let actual weight given = 100 ‚Äď 37 = 63% of the claimed weight So, shopkeeper gives only 63 units instead of 100, but charges for 100 Profit % = (100‚ąí6363)√ó100\left( \frac{100 - 63}{63} \right) \times 100(63100‚ąí63‚Äč)√ó100‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äč=(3763)√ó100=584663%= \left( \frac{37}{63} \right) \times 100 \\= 58 \frac{46}{63}\%=(6337‚Äč)√ó100=586346‚Äč%

questions.question: ‚ÄčIf x+1x=17 then x2+1x2 is:\text{If }x + \frac{ 1}{x} = 17 \text{ then }x^2 + \frac{ 1}{x^2} \text{ is:}If x+x1‚Äč=17 then x2+x21‚Äč is:
‚ÄčThe average weight of 26 students is 68 kg. If one new student is added, the average weight becomes 70 kg. What is the weight of the new student?‚Äč
questions.explanation: Given: The average weight of 26 students = 68 kg After adding one new student, average becomes = 70 kg We are required to find the weight of the new student. All Concept/Formula Used in Question: Formula: Total weight = Average √ó Number of students Weight of new student = Total weight after adding - Total original weight Solution: Total weight of 26 students = 68 √ó 26 = 1768 kg Total weight of 27 students = 70 √ó 27 = 1890 kg Weight of new student = 1890 - 1768 = 122 kg

questions.question: 15 chairs and 3 tables cost ‚āĻ7,800 and 14 chairs and 6 tables cost ‚āĻ9,200. What is the cost of 17 chairs and 18 tables?
questions.explanation: Given: 15 chairs and 3 tables cost ‚āĻ7,800. 14 chairs and 6 tables cost ‚āĻ9,200. We need to find the cost of 17 chairs and 18 tables. Solution: Let the cost of one chair be x and the cost of one table be y From the given condition; 15x + 3y = 7800.......(1) 14x + 6y = 9200........(2) Subtracting 2√ó\times√ó‚Äč (1) - (2) ‚Äč30x+6y=15600‚ąí14x‚ąí6y=‚ąí9200‚Äĺ16x=6400 x=40030x + 6y = 15600 \\\underline{-14x - 6y = -9200} \\ 16x \quad =\quad 6400 \\ \ \\x = 40030x+6y=15600‚ąí14x‚ąí6y=‚ąí9200‚Äč16x=6400 x=400‚Äč‚Äč Substituting x = 400 into the first equation to find y: 15(400) + 3y = 7800 3y = 7800 - 6000 = 1800 y = 600 Now, the cost of 17 chairs and 18 tables: = 17x + 18y = 17(400) + 18(600) = 6800 + 10800 = ‚āĻ17600

questions.question: ‚ÄčSimplify: 712‚ąí[114√∑(114‚ąí12)]\text{Simplify: } 7 \frac{1}{2} - \left[ 1 \frac{1}{4} \div \left( 1 \frac{1}{4} - \frac{1}{2} \right) \right]Simplify: 721‚Äč‚ąí[141‚Äč√∑(141‚Äč‚ąí21‚Äč)]‚Äč‚Äč
questions.explanation: Given: ‚Äč ‚Äč712‚ąí[114√∑(114‚ąí12)] 7 \frac{1}{2} - \left[ 1 \frac{1}{4} \div \left( 1 \frac{1}{4} - \frac{1}{2} \right) \right] 721‚Äč‚ąí[141‚Äč√∑(141‚Äč‚ąí21‚Äč)] Concept Used: ‚Äč Operation preference wise Symbol Brackets [], {}, () Orders, of x (power), ‚ąö (root), of Division √∑ Multiplication √ó Addition + Subtraction - Solution: ‚Äč ‚Äč712‚ąí[114√∑(114‚ąí12)] =152‚ąí[54√∑(54‚ąí24)] =152‚ąí[54√ó(43)] =152‚ąí[53] =45‚ąí106 =356 7 \frac{1}{2} - \left[ 1 \frac{1}{4} \div \left( 1 \frac{1}{4} - \frac{1}{2} \right) \right] \\ \ \\ = \frac{15}{2} - \left[ \frac{5}{4} \div \left( \frac{5}{4} - \frac{2}{4} \right) \right] \\ \ \\ = \frac{15}{2} - \left[ \frac{5}{4} \times \left( \frac{4}{3} \right) \right] \\ \ \\ = \frac{15}{2} - \left[ \frac{5}{3} \right] \\ \ \\ = \frac{45-10}{6} \\ \ \\ = \frac{35}{6}721‚Äč‚ąí[141‚Äč√∑(141‚Äč‚ąí21‚Äč)] =215‚Äč‚ąí[45‚Äč√∑(45‚Äč‚ąí42‚Äč)] =215‚Äč‚ąí[45‚Äč√ó(34‚Äč)] =215‚Äč‚ąí[35‚Äč] =645‚ąí10‚Äč =635‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: Find the product of the greatest among the 0.82, 0.802, 0.85, 0.085 numbers with the smallest among the numbers 0.3, 0.03, 0.203, 2.03
questions.explanation: Given: Two groups of numbers: Group A: 0.82, 0.802, 0.85, 0.085 Group B: 0.3, 0.03, 0.203, 2.03 Solution: The greatest in Group A Compare: 0.82, 0.802, 0.85, 0.085 Greatest = 0.85 The smallest in Group B Compare: 0.3, 0.03, 0.203, 2.03 Smallest = 0.03 Now, the product = 0.85 √ó 0.03 = 0.0255

questions.question: The x and y co-ordinates of six points are tabulated as below. Observe the table carefully and answer the given question. Points X-co-ordinate Y-co-ordinate A 15 42 B 20 46 C 18 44 D 16 48 E 21 50 F 22 52 Find the distance between F and C.
questions.explanation: Given: Coordinates of point F = (22, 52) Coordinates of point C = (18, 44) Formula Used: Distance between two points (x‚āĀ, y‚āĀ) and (x‚āā, y‚āā) = (x2‚ąíx1)2+(y2‚ąíy1)2\sqrt{(x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2}(x2‚Äč‚ąíx1‚Äč)2+(y2‚Äč‚ąíy1‚Äč)2‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: Substitute the values for point F (x‚āĀ, y‚āĀ) = (22, 52) and point C (x‚āā, y‚āā) = (18, 44): ‚ÄčDistance=(18‚ąí22)2+(44‚ąí52)2Distance=(‚ąí4)2+(‚ąí8)2=16+64=80 units\text{Distance} = \sqrt{(18 - 22)^2 + (44 - 52)^2}\\\text{Distance} = \sqrt{(-4)^2 + (-8)^2} = \sqrt{16 + 64} = \sqrt{80} \, \text{units}Distance=(18‚ąí22)2+(44‚ąí52)2‚ÄčDistance=(‚ąí4)2+(‚ąí8)2‚Äč=16+64‚Äč=80‚Äčunits‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: A reservoir is in the shape of a frustum of a right circular cone. It is 26 m across at the top and 2 m across at the bottom. It is 5 m deep. Find the area of its curved surface. (Take ŌÄ = 227\frac{22}{7}722‚Äč‚Äč)
questions.explanation: Given: Diameter at the top = 26 m, so the radius at the top r1 = 262\frac{26}2226‚Äč‚Äč = 13 m Diameter at the bottom = 2 m, so the radius at the bottom r2 =22\frac{2}222‚Äč‚Äč = 1 m Depth of the frustum (h) = 5 m Formula Used: Curved Surface Area of frustrum = ŌÄ(r1 + r2)l where l= (r1‚ąír2)2+h2\sqrt{(r_1 - r_2)^2 + h^2}(r1‚Äč‚ąír2‚Äč)2+h2‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Solution: ‚Äčl=(r1‚ąír2)2+h2l=(13‚ąí1)2+52=122+52=144+25=169=13 mArea=ŌÄ(r1+r2)l=227√ó(13+1)√ó13=227√ó14√ó13A=227√ó182A=40047=572 m2The area of the curved surface is 572 m2l = \sqrt{(r_1 - r_2)^2 + h^2} \\l = \sqrt{(13 - 1)^2 + 5^2} = \sqrt{12^2 + 5^2} = \sqrt{144 + 25} = \sqrt{169} = 13 \, \text{m} \\\text{Area} = \pi (r_1 + r_2) l = \frac{22}{7} \times (13 + 1) \times 13 = \frac{22}{7} \times 14 \times 13 \\A = \frac{22}{7} \times 182 \\A = \frac{4004}{7} = 572 \, \text{m}^2 \\\text{The area of the curved surface is } 572 \, \text{m}^2l=(r1‚Äč‚ąír2‚Äč)2+h2‚Äčl=(13‚ąí1)2+52‚Äč=122+52‚Äč=144+25‚Äč=169‚Äč=13mArea=ŌÄ(r1‚Äč+r2‚Äč)l=722‚Äč√ó(13+1)√ó13=722‚Äč√ó14√ó13A=722‚Äč√ó182A=74004‚Äč=572m2The area of the curved surface is 572m2‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: Evaluate cos2(45o+őł)+cos2(45o‚ąíőł)cosec230osin245o‚ąísec260o\frac{cos^2 (45^o + \theta) + cos^2 (45^o - \theta)}{cosec^2 30^o sin^2 45^o - sec^2 60^o}cosec230osin245o‚ąísec260ocos2(45o+őł)+cos2(45o‚ąíőł)‚Äč.‚Äč
questions.explanation: Given: ‚Äčcos‚Ā°2(45‚ąė+őł)+cos‚Ā°2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)cosec‚Ā°230‚ąė‚čÖsin‚Ā°245‚ąė‚ąísec‚Ā°260‚ąė\frac{\cos^2(45^\circ + \theta) + \cos^2(45^\circ - \theta)}{\cosec^2 30^\circ \cdot \sin^2 45^\circ - \sec^2 60^\circ}cosec230‚ąė‚čÖsin245‚ąė‚ąísec260‚ąėcos2(45‚ąė+őł)+cos2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Concept Used: Complementary Angle Theorem: cos‚Ā°(90‚ąė‚ąíx)=sin‚Ā°x, sin‚Ā°(90‚ąė‚ąíx)=cos‚Ā°x\cos(90^\circ - x) = \sin x, \ \ \sin(90^\circ - x) = \cos xcos(90‚ąė‚ąíx)=sinx, sin(90‚ąė‚ąíx)=cosx‚Äč‚Äč Formula Used: ‚Äčcos‚Ā°2(45‚ąė+őł)=sin‚Ā°2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)\cos^2(45^\circ + \theta) = \sin^2(45^\circ - \theta)cos2(45‚ąė+őł)=sin2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äčcos‚Ā°2(45‚ąė+őł)+cos‚Ā°2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)=sin‚Ā°2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)+cos‚Ā°2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)=1\cos^2(45^\circ + \theta) + \cos^2(45^\circ - \theta) = \sin^2(45^\circ - \theta) + \cos^2(45^\circ - \theta) = 1cos2(45‚ąė+őł)+cos2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)=sin2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)+cos2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)=1‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äčcosec‚Ā°30‚ąė=2=&gtcosec‚Ā°230‚ąė=4\cosec 30^\circ = 2 \Rightarrow \cosec^2 30^\circ = 4cosec30‚ąė=2=&gtcosec230‚ąė=4‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äčsin‚Ā°245‚ąė=(12)2=12\sin^2 45^\circ = \left( \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \right)^2 = \frac{1}{2}sin245‚ąė=(2‚Äč1‚Äč)2=21‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč ‚Äčsec‚Ā°60‚ąė=2=&gtsec‚Ā°260‚ąė=4\sec 60^\circ = 2 \Rightarrow \sec^2 60^\circ = 4sec60‚ąė=2=&gtsec260‚ąė=4‚Äč‚Äč Solution: ‚Äčcos‚Ā°2(45‚ąė+őł)+cos‚Ā°2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)cosec‚Ā°230‚ąė‚čÖsin‚Ā°245‚ąė‚ąísec‚Ā°260‚ąė =cos‚Ā°2(45‚ąė+őł)+sin‚Ā°2(90‚ąí(45‚ąė‚ąíőł))cosec‚Ā°230‚ąė‚čÖsin‚Ā°245‚ąė‚ąísec‚Ā°260‚ąė =cos‚Ā°2(45‚ąė+őł)+sin‚Ā°2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)cosec‚Ā°230‚ąė‚čÖsin‚Ā°245‚ąė‚ąísec‚Ā°260‚ąė =1(2)2‚čÖ(12)2‚ąí(2)2 =12‚ąí4=‚ąí12\frac{\cos^2(45^\circ + \theta) + \cos^2(45^\circ - \theta)}{\cosec^2 30^\circ \cdot \sin^2 45^\circ - \sec^2 60^\circ} \\ \ \\ = \frac{\cos^2(45^\circ + \theta) + \sin^2(90-(45^\circ - \theta))}{\cosec^2 30^\circ \cdot \sin^2 45^\circ - \sec^2 60^\circ}\\ \ \\= \frac{\cos^2(45^\circ + \theta) + \sin^2(45^\circ - \theta)}{\cosec^2 30^\circ \cdot \sin^2 45^\circ - \sec^2 60^\circ}\\ \ \\= \frac{1}{(2)^2 \cdot \left(\frac{1}{\sqrt2}\right)^2 - (2)^2}\\ \ \\ = \frac{1}{2 - 4} = \frac{-1}{2}cosec230‚ąė‚čÖsin245‚ąė‚ąísec260‚ąėcos2(45‚ąė+őł)+cos2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)‚Äč =cosec230‚ąė‚čÖsin245‚ąė‚ąísec260‚ąėcos2(45‚ąė+őł)+sin2(90‚ąí(45‚ąė‚ąíőł))‚Äč =cosec230‚ąė‚čÖsin245‚ąė‚ąísec260‚ąėcos2(45‚ąė+őł)+sin2(45‚ąė‚ąíőł)‚Äč =(2)2‚čÖ(2‚Äč1‚Äč)2‚ąí(2)21‚Äč =2‚ąí41‚Äč=2‚ąí1‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč

questions.question: The perpendicular length from the origin to the line 6x + 8y - 48 = 0 is:
questions.explanation: Given: The equation of the line is 6x + 8y - 48 = 0 Concept Used: The perpendicular distance from a point (x0,y0)(x_0, y_0)(x0‚Äč,y0‚Äč)‚Äč to a line ax + by + c = 0 is given by the formula: Distance = ‚ą£ax0+by0+c‚ą£a2+b2 \frac{|ax_0 + by_0 + c|}{\sqrt{a^2 + b^2}}a2+b2‚Äč‚ą£ax0‚Äč+by0‚Äč+c‚ą£‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Here, the point is the origin (0, 0), so x0=0x_0 = 0x0‚Äč=0‚Äč and y0=0y_0 = 0y0‚Äč=0‚Äč‚Äč Solution: the line equation 6x + 8y - 48 = 0 in the form ax + by + c = 0: a = 6 , b = 8, c = -48 Perpendicular distance from (0, 0) to the line: Distance = ‚ą£0+0+(‚ąí48)‚ą£62+82 \frac{|0 + 0 +(-48)|}{\sqrt{6^2 + 8^2}}62+82‚Äč‚ą£0+0+(‚ąí48)‚ą£‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Distance = ‚ą£(‚ąí48)‚ą£36+64\frac{|(-48)|}{\sqrt{36 + 64}}36+64‚Äč‚ą£(‚ąí48)‚ą£‚Äč‚Äč‚Äč Distance = 4810\frac{48}{10}1048‚Äč‚Äč = 4.8 units. Thus, the perpendicular distance from the origin to the line is 4.8 units.
